questions.prompt: After changing a valid writable page-table entry to read-only, why may a TLB invalidation be required?

choices.A: The disk block cache always contains the old page
choices.B: A cached translation may still contain the old writable permission
choices.C: Invalidation changes the physical page contents
choices.D: Only to make the page table smaller

questions.explanation: The TLB caches both address and permission decision. Without the architecture's required invalidation, the old writable entry can continue authorizing stores.

questions.prompt: What must a kernel do before copying n bytes from a user pointer?

choices.A: Check only that the first byte is mapped
choices.B: Trust pointers from a process that already opened a file
choices.C: Validate overflow, every covered mapping, and required user permissions for the complete range

questions.explanation: A range can wrap or cross into an unmapped or supervisor page. The complete operation and permission must be proven before or during a fault-safe copy.

questions.prompt: After fork, two descriptors refer to the same open-file description. What state is normally shared?

choices.B: The current file offset and open status
choices.C: The entire virtual address space forever
choices.D: The child's exit code before exit

questions.explanation: Descriptor tables are per process, but duplicated descriptors can reference one open-file description, including its offset and status. Reference counts preserve its lifetime.

questions.prompt: What prevents a corrupted FAT cluster chain from looping forever?

choices.A: Assuming every image was cleanly unmounted
choices.B: A traversal bound plus cycle detection over validated cluster numbers
choices.C: Reading the same entry until it changes
choices.D: Treating every zero byte as end-of-file

questions.explanation: All on-disk metadata is untrusted. Range validation, a cluster-count step bound, and explicit cycle detection give deterministic safe failure.

questions.prompt: Why can a pipeline reader wait forever if the parent retains an unused copy of the pipe write end?

choices.A: The reader sees EOF only after every write-end reference is closed
choices.C: The scheduler refuses to run readers
choices.D: The shell must keep every descriptor open

questions.explanation: EOF is an ownership fact: as long as any write endpoint remains open, more bytes could arrive. Every process must close pipe ends it does not own.
